element_introduction_order.element: Basic lemming spawning and exit mechanics
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn that lemmings spawn from trap doors and must reach a designated exit
Vertically scaling obstacles; players discover climbers fall if they hit overhangs
Safe descent from heights using parachute mechanic
Lemmings reverse direction when hitting a blocker; used for crowd control and positioning
Constructs 12-step stairways; stops when hitting head, running out of bricks, or solid objects
Horizontal tunneling through terrain; cannot break steel barriers
Diagonal downward tunneling; introduces angled terrain manipulation
Straight downward tunneling; completes directional digging toolkit
5-second timer before explosion; used for destructive landscape clearing; can kill the lemming
Terrain type that cannot be breached by bashers, miners, or diggers; forces strategic routing
Environmental threats requiring bridge-building or alternate routes
Heights that kill lemmings; players learn safe drop distances via trial
Level-specific instant-kill obstacles requiring avoidance or careful timing
Players learn time pressure; all remaining lemmings explode if timer expires
Varies per level; forces strategic skill assignment decisions
Players can adjust lemming spawn rate from initial setting up to 99; slowing essential for precision

typical_relief_triggers: Blocker positioning preventing unwanted lemming loss
Pausing to plan next skill assignment
Transition to next level after meeting save percentage
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple lemmings falling toward hazards simultaneously
Limited skill uses remaining for complex terrain
Lemming heading toward fall or trap with no escape route visible
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Asynchronous real-time with pause; tension builds as lemming wave approaches obstacles; relief comes from successful routing through danger zones; time limit creates persistent pressure throughout level

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Unreachable areas or secret passages not required for exit but containing bonus lemmings

difficulty_curve.early_game: Fun rating introduces core skills (climber, floater, blocker, builder) in isolation or simple combinations; low time pressure; generous save percentages
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tricky rating combines multiple skills; introduces bashers, miners, diggers; moderate time pressure; requires strategic sequencing of skill assignments; tighter save thresholds
difficulty_curve.late_game: Taxing and Mayhem ratings feature all skills including bomber; introduces steel barriers; tight time limits; high precision required; complex multi-stage solutions; save percentages increase to 90%+ or specific counts
notable_spikes: Transition from Fun to Tricky: first encounter with skill combinations and terrain variety
Taxing rating introduction: bomber skill and time pressure intensify
Taxing 21 (All the 6's/Ohayo Lemming san): infamous level with challenging spatial layout and strict timing
Mayhem rating: extreme difficulty spike; demands mastery of all mechanics and precision timing

notable_level_patterns.description: Lemmings must climb or build upward to reach exit; falling from incorrect height kills them; requires climber or builder skill with careful application
Lemmings blocked by solid terrain; player must designate bashers, miners, or diggers to create passage; multiple solution paths possible
Long pathway with multiple traps, lava, or fall dangers requiring multiple skill assignments to navigate safely
Uses blockers and release rate adjustments to manage lemming crowds, preventing premature deaths or wasted skill uses
Bomber skills required to clear large destructible barriers within tight time constraints; miscalculation of bomb placement results in level failure

sources_used: tcrf - Lemmings (SNES) The Cutting Room Floor (regional differences, level changes, unused content documentation)
wikipedia - Lemmings (video game) (gameplay mechanics, skill descriptions, difficulty structure, objective overview)
